mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales (RSAFS) indicate robust consumer demand, which lifts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The Spearman rank correlation between monthly RSAFS growth and 20-day forward SPX returns is less than or equal to 0 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 5.5%, it signals labor market weakness that reduces consumer spending power and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In all months where UNRATE exceeds 5.5, the mean 20-day forward SPX return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in industrial production (INDPRO) and acceleration in consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) creates stagflationary conditions that compress profit margins and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls and CPIAUCSL rises over a rolling 3-month window
failure_condition: Episodes with simultaneous 3-month decline in INDPRO and rise in CPIAUCSL produce negative 20-day forward SPX returns in less than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, when the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) has already increased by more than 0.5% over the prior 6 months, a further 25 basis point increase tightens financial conditions and depresses equity prices over the following 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increased by at least 25bp within the last 30 days
failure_condition: In tightening cycle months (FEDFUNDS +0.5% over 6 months), the average 20-day forward return following a 25bp hike is not negative (one-sided z-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding real economic activity, which boosts corporate earnings and supports higher equity prices over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: The Spearman rank correlation between quarterly INDPRO growth and subsequent 60-day SPX returns is less than or equal to 0 over any rolling 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) fall below 2000 thousand units, it signals a sharp deceleration in the housing market that weakens consumer confidence and near-term economic activity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In months where HOUST < 2000, the subsequent 5-day forward SPX return is negative in fewer than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in both nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicate a broad economic contraction that sharply reduces corporate earnings and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines and RSAFS declines month-over-month
failure_condition: When both PAYEMS and RSAFS decline in the same month, the subsequent 60-day forward SPX return is negative in less than 70% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation regime defined by CPIAUCSL exceeding 200, a monthly CPI acceleration of more than 0.5% reinforces expectations of tighter monetary policy and erodes real earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In high inflation regime months (CPIAUCSL > 200), the average 20-day forward SPX return following a monthly CPI rise >0.5% is not negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).
